2604.11467.title: From Attribution to Action: A Human-Centered Application of Activation Steering
2604.11467.paper_summary: Explainable AI (XAI) methods reveal which features influence model predictions, yet provide limited means for practitioners to act on these explanations. Activation steering of components identified via XAI offers a path toward actionable explanations, although its practical utility remains understudied. We introduce an interactive workflow combining SAE-based attribution with activation steering for instance-level analysis of concept usage in vision models, implemented as a web-based tool. Based on this workflow, we conduct semi-structured expert interviews (N=8) with debugging tasks on CLIP to investigate how practitioners reason about, trust, and apply activation steering. We find that steering enables a shift from inspection to intervention-based hypothesis testing (8/8 participants), with most grounding trust in observed model responses rather than explanation plausibility alone (6/8). Participants adopted systematic debugging strategies dominated by component suppression (7/8) and highlighted risks including ripple effects and limited generalization of instance-level corrections. Overall, activation steering renders interpretability more actionable while raising important considerations for safe and effective use.
2604.11467.paper_summary_zh: 可解釋的人工智慧（XAI）方法揭示了哪些特徵影響模型預測，但為從業者提供的行動方式有限。通過 XAI 確定的組件的激活引導提供了一條通向可行解釋的途徑，儘管其實際效用仍然未受到充分研究。我們介紹了一種互動工作流程，將基於 SAE 的歸因與激活引導相結合，用於視覺模型中概念使用的實例級分析，並實現為一個基於網頁的工具。基於這一工作流程，我們對 CLIP 進行了半結構化的專家訪談（N=8），並進行了調試任務，以調查從業者如何推理、信任和應用激活引導。我們發現，激活引導使得檢查轉變為基於干預的假設測試（8/8 參與者），大多數人將信任基於觀察到的模型反應，而不僅僅是解釋的合理性（6/8）。參與者採用了以組件抑制為主的系統化調試策略（7/8），並強調了包括漣漪效應和實例級修正的有限泛化等風險。總體而言，激活引導使得可解釋性更具可行性，同時提出了安全和有效使用的重要考量。
2604.11467.author: Tobias Labarta et.al.
2604.11467.authors: Tobias Labarta, Maximilian Dreyer, Katharina Weitz, Wojciech Samek, Sebastian Lapuschkin
